purpose: Handover context for the TorontoGuessr session. Written before a chat compaction so work can resume cleanly. Safe to delete once no longer needed (it is not part of the app).

project.name: TorontoGuessr
project.summary: Toronto-only Street View guessing game. Next.js 15 App Router frontend, small Node backend (now TypeScript) deployed as Vercel Functions, Supabase (PostgreSQL) for persistence, Google Maps JS API + Street View for gameplay.

deploys.backend: https://toronto-guessr-backend.vercel.app (Vercel project, root dir = backend). API base is /api.
deploys.note: Two independent Vercel projects, both auto-deploy from main. Frontend NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL points at the backend /api.

identity_and_git.commit_convention: Conventional-commit style, lowercase, no em dashes anywhere in repo. Do NOT add Co-Authored-By / any AI attribution — user wants everything under their name only.
identity_and_git.current_branch: results-map-reuse (branched off main @ 0952a41, CLEAN — no commits, no uncommitted changes; O4 was NOT started beyond reading files)
identity_and_git.pr_flow: For each unit of work: branch off main -> implement -> verify (tsc + vitest + frontend build + local server smoke) -> commit -> `git push -u origin <branch>` -> `gh pr create` -> `gh pr merge <n> --squash --admin --delete-branch` -> `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main`. Merges are squash, under the user's name.

current_request.instruction: User asked to implement 6 improvements, EACH as its own PR that I both create AND merge under their name, one at a time.
the_six_items: O1 Observability: per-request router timing logs (total/db/app) + Vercel Speed Insights on frontend
O2 Region pinning + guess-scoring RPC: pin backend region to Supabase's; collapse guess read+write into one Postgres call
O3 Rate limiting /games/start: per-IP limiter to deter session spam
O4 Results-map reuse: unify guess map + results map into ONE persistent Google Map instance (they currently remount each round)
O5 Frontend tests + ESLint: vitest for pure fns (share text, formatDay, daysSinceLaunch, cn) + ESLint (next config); wire into CI
O6 Streaks: localStorage daily-play streak counter shown on summary + landing hero

status.O4: IN PROGRESS / DECISION PENDING — see o4_decision below. Branch results-map-reuse created but nothing implemented yet.

o4_decision.problem: The guessing map (in components/guess-panel.tsx) and the results map (in components/game-results.tsx) are separate GameMap -> GoogleMap mounts rendered in different top-level branches of frontend/app/game/page.tsx (isPlaying vs !isPlaying), so React fully remounts the google.maps.Map on every guess<->results transition (~2 map mounts per round).
o4_decision.reality_check: useJsApiLoader caches the Maps script as a singleton and tiles are browser-cached, so the remount is cheap (~100-200ms map-object reconstruction, no script reload). The heavy object (StreetViewPanorama) is ALREADY reused via S3.
options_presented_to_user: A) Safe partial reuse: keep the immersive stage mounted (hidden) during results so the guessing map + panorama are created once and reused across all 5 rounds. Zero UX change, low risk. Results keeps its own map.

B) Full single-map rewrite: unify guessing + results into one stage sharing a single keyed GameMap instance; preserves results UX via conditional classes but is a sizable, higher-regression-risk rewrite of the flagship screen; verify live in-browser.
C) Skip O4 and move to O5/O6 (remount is cheap; panorama already reused).
o4_decision.recommendation: A or C. B is correct architecture but disproportionate risk/effort for the ROI. The AskUserQuestion asking this was interrupted; ASK THE USER which option before implementing O4.
o4_decision.constraints_learned: Any shared map must NOT live inside a component that unmounts between states (GuessPanel/GameResults). True single-instance requires either (A) keeping the stage mounted, (B) a keyed <GameMap> at a stable JSX position with both layouts inlined into the page (delete GuessPanel/GameResults, port their verdict/submit chrome), or an imperative detached-DOM singleton (more logic duplication).

O5_frontend_tests_and_eslint.tests: Add vitest to the frontend workspace. Test pure functions: the share-text builder (components/share-results.tsx — extract the text builder to a pure fn if inline), app/stats/stats-client.tsx formatDay + daysSinceLaunch (extract to a lib module so they're importable/testable), lib/utils.ts cn. Put tests in frontend/tests or colocated *.test.ts.
O5_frontend_tests_and_eslint.eslint: Frontend currently has NO lint config. Add eslint + eslint-config-next (matching next 15) + a lint script. Fix real errors; the shadcn components in components/ui/* may produce warnings — keep them non-blocking or scope config. Consider `next lint` vs flat config; whatever integrates cleanly with next 15.2.8.
O5_frontend_tests_and_eslint.ci: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml frontend job to also run frontend tests (and optionally lint). Backend job already runs tsc + vitest.
O5_frontend_tests_and_eslint.risk: ESLint can surface many pre-existing warnings; keep it from blocking the build. Verify `npm run build --workspace frontend` still passes.

O6_streaks.goal: Wordle-style daily-play streak counter in localStorage, shown on the game summary screen and the landing hero.
O6_streaks.design: New client util (e.g. frontend/lib/streak.ts) with pure, testable functions: given the stored {lastPlayedDate, streak} and today's Toronto date key, compute the updated streak (increment if yesterday, reset to 1 if gap, unchanged if same day). Use the same Toronto date-key logic as stats (America/Toronto). Update the streak when a game is FINISHED (summary state in app/game/page.tsx). Show a flame emoji + count on the summary hero and on the landing page hero (app/page.tsx).
O6_streaks.notes: Pure streak math should get vitest coverage (pairs well with O5). localStorage only (no backend/accounts). Consider tying increment to finishing a game (not merely starting).

pending_manual_migrations_in_supabase.note: Backend uses PostgREST (service-role key); it CANNOT run DDL. These SQL files must be pasted into the Supabase SQL editor by the user. All backend code falls back gracefully until applied, so merging is always safe. There is NO local Postgres in this environment, so SQL functions cannot be tested here.
files: backend/supabase/add_stats_function_and_indexes.sql — daily_game_stats() aggregate + leaderboard indexes (from S2). Timing logs show /leaderboard doing ~1s reads, suggesting the indexes may NOT be applied yet — user should confirm.
backend/supabase/add_pick_game_rounds_function.sql — pick_game_rounds() sampler (from S2). Falls back to full-table scan until applied.

backend/supabase/add_submit_guess_function.sql — submit_guess() atomic scoring (from O2). ONLY used when backend env GUESS_RPC_ENABLED=true. Leave the flag unset until the migration is applied AND verified with a real game (it is a faithful but locally-untested SQL port of the scoring/deadline loop).

O1_observability.how: AsyncLocalStorage.enterWith binds a per-request timings object; supabase.ts adds each round trip (read=GET/HEAD, write=POST/PATCH/DELETE); logs one line `[timing] METHOD path status total=..ms db=..ms(r/w/n) app=..ms` on finish. Parallel DB calls can make summed db exceed total (app clamps to 0).

files: backend/src/game-store.ts (submit_guess RPC path gated by GUESS_RPC_ENABLED, zod-validated, JS fallback)
O2_region_and_guess_rpc.region_assumption: Pinned iad1 (US-East, observed deploy region, common Supabase region). If Supabase is elsewhere, change vercel.json regions.
O2_region_and_guess_rpc.guess_rpc: Default OFF (GUESS_RPC_ENABLED unset) => byte-identical JS path. When on with function absent => logs warning + JS fallback (verified). SQL itself untested (no local PG).

O3_rate_limit.limitation: In-memory per serverless instance (not a global cap); deters single-client scripting. Injectable clock for deterministic tests.

backend_architecture_notes.language: TypeScript, ESM. moduleResolution NodeNext -> ALL relative imports MUST end in .js (e.g. import { x } from './foo.js'). This is required for Vercel's per-file ESM compilation; forgetting it caused a prod outage (ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND).
backend_architecture_notes.vercel_routing: backend/api/index.ts is the SINGLE function. backend/vercel.json rewrites /api/:path* -> /api?path=/:path*, and src/router.ts recovers the original path from the `path` query param (falls back to url.pathname for local/tests). Do NOT re-introduce per-route api/*.ts files.
backend_architecture_notes.router: src/router.ts — switch on method+pathname, zod-validates inputs, admin routes gated by x-admin-token / Bearer vs ADMIN_REVIEW_TOKEN. Leaderboard + stats set Cache-Control (CDN). Timing + rate-limit wired in.
backend_architecture_notes.data_layer: src/supabase.ts — generic PostgREST client (selectRows<T>, insertRow<T>, updateSingleRow<T>, countRows, deleteRows<T>, callRpc<T>). 1000-row response cap is a recurring gotcha; SQL aggregates/samplers exist to avoid it.
backend_architecture_notes.game_store: src/game-store.ts — sessions, scoring (via scoring-service.ts), leaderboard, stats. submitGuess has: 60s limit + 15s grace deadline (ROUND_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS/ROUND_DEADLINE_GRACE_SECONDS), schema fallback for optional columns (mode/challenge_date/round_started_at via withSessionSchemaFallback + GAME_SESSION_COLUMNS_EXTENDED/LEGACY), next-round prefetch in the response (nextRound), optimistic-concurrency update filter.
backend_architecture_notes.location_service: src/services/location-service.ts — selectGameRounds(count, seed) prefers pick_game_rounds RPC, falls back to full scan. Daily challenge uses a deterministic seed from the date.
backend_architecture_notes.scoring: scoring-service.ts — haversine km; score: <=0.1km=5000, >=2km=0, else round(5000*(1-(d-0.1)/1.9)). Covered by backend/tests/scoring-service.test.ts.
backend_architecture_notes.env: backend/.env has SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY, ADMIN_REVIEW_TOKEN, LOCATION_GENERATION_ENABLED=false. New optional: GUESS_RPC_ENABLED (unset).

frontend_architecture_notes.framework: Next 15 App Router, React 19, Tailwind, shadcn/ui (components/ui/*), next-themes (default dark), Inter font. Design system: 'Cartographic Premium' — tokens in app/globals.css, shared chrome in components/site/*.
frontend_architecture_notes.gameplay: app/game/page.tsx state machine: loading/guessing/submitting/results/summary/error. Immersive stage (GamePanorama + floating GameHUD with RoundCountdown + GuessPanel) for guessing; GameResults (result card + comparison map) for results; summary hero + round breakdown + ShareResults + save username + play again. Next-round prefetch (currentResult.nextRound) + PanoPrefetch warm the next panorama.
frontend_architecture_notes.maps: components/game-map.tsx (GameMap) wraps @react-google-maps GoogleMap: theme-aware styles (useMapTheme light/dark, independent of site theme, localStorage), custom pin data URIs, fitBounds on results, an on-map light/dark toggle button. components/gamepanorama.tsx creates StreetViewPanorama with motionTracking:false.
frontend_architecture_notes.stats_page: app/stats/page.tsx + stats-client.tsx — recharts area chart, ranges 24h/7d/30d/All time (All time = days since 2026-04-01 launch). Uses fetchGameStats.
frontend_architecture_notes.logo: public/cntower-mark.png (transparent, background removed) rendered via components/site/brand-mark.tsx with dark:invert (black in light, white in dark). Original public/cntowerlogo.webp is unused. Footer skyline: components/site/footer-backdrop.tsx uses public/toronto-skyline.png if present else vector Skyline.
frontend_architecture_notes.no_em_dashes: The whole repo is kept free of em/en dashes (— –). Keep it that way in code, comments, and README.

verification_recipes.backend_tests: cd backend && npx vitest run   (currently 37 tests across scoring/username/http-utils/rate-limit)
verification_recipes.frontend_build: cd frontend && NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=https://toronto-guessr-backend.vercel.app/api NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=ci-key npx next build
verification_recipes.backend_local_smoke: cd backend && (npx tsx src/server.ts &)  then curl localhost:3001/api/health, /api/leaderboard, /api/stats/games?days=7; POST /api/games/start then /api/games/:id/guess. Kill stale servers first: lsof -ti :3001 | xargs kill.
verification_recipes.live_gameplay_preview: Run backend (tsx src/server.ts) + frontend dev (via .claude/launch.json name 'frontend' on port 3210 through the Claude_Preview MCP tools) together; frontend .env already points at localhost:3001. Real Supabase + real Google key work. Use preview_start/preview_screenshot/preview_eval. Remember to remove any temp .claude/launch.json and stop servers when done; test sessions left in_progress do not hit the leaderboard.
verification_recipes.prod_smoke: curl https://toronto-guessr-backend.vercel.app/api/health etc. The .github/workflows/smoke.yml runs these post-deploy and every 15 min, opening an 'outage'-labeled issue on failure.

gotchas: ESM: every relative backend import ends in .js (NodeNext). Missing .js => prod ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND (invisible locally under tsx).
Backend is one Vercel function via api/index.ts + vercel.json rewrite; do not add per-route files.
No local Postgres: SQL migrations/functions can't be tested here; ship with fallbacks + flags and document manual apply.
PostgREST 1000-row cap: never fetch whole tables for counts/aggregation; use SQL functions.
Merging to main auto-deploys BOTH Vercel projects; smoke workflow validates post-deploy.
Two Vercel projects: frontend build in CI needs NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY env (placeholders ok).
CountUp/animations honor prefers-reduced-motion; CountUp also has a settle-timeout fallback for background tabs.
Bash tool: cd in compound commands can prompt; use absolute paths / cd at start. `lsof -ti :3001 | xargs kill` to free the local backend port.
This handover.json lives in repo root — add to .gitignore or delete before committing so it isn't shipped.

task_tracker_ids.note: Harness TaskCreate ids for this session (may not survive compaction).

immediate_next_step: Resolve the O4 decision (ask user: option A safe-partial, B full-rewrite, or C skip). Then implement O4 (or skip), then O5 (frontend tests + ESLint), then O6 (streaks) — each as its own branch -> PR -> squash-merge under the user's name, verifying tsc/tests/build before each merge.
